invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Authentication commands (USER and PASS) must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE) are issued.
A data connection establishment command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) should be issued prior to any data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
The RNTO command must follow an RNFR command to complete a file renaming operation.
If authentication fails, the client may retry the authentication sequence starting with USER.
A PASS command without a preceding USER command typically results in a 530 Not Logged In error.
USER.username: Must contain the username string.
PASS.password: Must contain the password string.
TYPE.type-code: Must be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image (Binary).
CWD.path: Must specify the path to the directory to change to.
RETR.filename: Must specify the filename to retrieve.
STOR.filename: Must specify the filename to store.
SIZE.filename: Must specify the file name for which size is requested.
RNFR.filename: Must specify the old filename to be renamed.
RNTO.filename: Must specify the new filename for the rename operation.

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, APPE, CDUP, DELE, FEAT, HELP, MKD, MLSD

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

Authentication commands (USER and PASS) must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE) are issued.
A data connection establishment command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) should be issued prior to any data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR).

USER.username: Must contain the username string.
PASS.password: Must contain the password string.
TYPE.type-code: Must be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image (Binary).
CWD.path: Must specify the path to the directory to change to.
RETR.filename: Must specify the filename to retrieve.

status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP handshake; response code 331 indicates password required.
Response code 230 confirms successful login.
Response code 530 indicates failure but allows the session to remain in the authentication phase for retry.
Metadata operations like PWD (257) do not change the authentication state.
Navigation operations like CWD (250) do not change the authentication state.
PASV (227) is the standard method to enter passive mode for data transfer.
PORT (200) sets up the active mode data connection.
While usually preceded by PASV/PORT, a direct transition is possible via default ports or implicit channels as noted in traces (150).
LIST (150) initiates the transfer once the channel is ready.
RETR (150) initiates the transfer once the channel is ready.
Issuing commands like PWD after a transfer confirms the return to the idle authenticated state.
Issuing status commands after a transfer confirms the return to the authenticated state.
RNFR (350) marks the start of the rename operation.
RNTO (250) completes the rename and returns to the authenticated state.
The REIN command is designed to reset the session state.
After a reset, a new authentication sequence must begin.
QUIT (221) closes the session.
QUIT can be issued before authentication to close the connection.
EPSV is the extended version of PASV and functions similarly for establishing data channels.
EPRT is the extended version of PORT and functions similarly for establishing data channels.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 and the provided documentation explicitly state that PASS must follow USER.

status_changes.description: A data connection establishment command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) should be issued prior to any data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR).
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP behavior requires setting up the data channel before issuing commands that utilize it.

status_changes.description: The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
status_changes.rationale: Standard protocol behavior and documented traces show QUIT being used at various stages.

status_changes.description: If authentication fails, the client may retry the authentication sequence starting with USER.
status_changes.rationale: Trace evidence from Session 2 specifically demonstrates this retry sequence.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: Must specify the path to the directory to change to.
status_changes.rationale: Standard parameter for directory navigation.

status_changes.description: RETR.filename: Must specify the filename to retrieve.
status_changes.rationale: Standard parameter for file retrieval.

status_changes.description: STOR.filename: Must specify the filename to store.
status_changes.rationale: Standard parameter for file storage.

status_changes.description: SIZE.filename: Must specify the file name for which size is requested.
status_changes.rationale: Standard parameter for the SIZE command extension.
